title: Medical - NREMT Medical/Behavioral/Geriatric Exam Questions
description: Medical assessment and behavioral health questions from NREMT database covering stroke assessment, seizures, diabetes, behavioral emergencies, geriatric care, and respiratory conditions

questions.text: The parts to check in the Cincinnati prehospital stroke scale are :
Asthma that resolves in 1-2 hours spontaneously or by bronchodilators refers to which phase of asthma :
Asthma that does not respond to bronchodilators so the patient need to an anti inflammatory medication this phase refers to:
The patients with COPD are switched to a hypoxic drive they breathe based on high levels of O2:
All seizures are life threatening injuries:
Postictal state is a term refers to a patients complains of:
options: Sleep and unresponsive after a seizure

questions.text: One of the following is not a part of Cincinnati pre hospital stroke scale :
One of the following is not a common cause of seizure :
You are treating a patient who reports that she has COPD. If she has emphysema, you would expect:
Chronic bronchitis is most commonly associated with exposure to:
A behavioral emergency is best defined as a:
options: Hormonal imbalance leading to disturbed thinking patterns
Metabolic emergency that jeopardizes the patient's health and welfare
Chemical imbalance in the brain that affects a person's medical well-being
Change in mood or behavior that cannot be tolerated by the involved person or others

questions.text: While you are gathering a medical history, your patient reports that she takes Zoloft, leading you to ask whether the patient has a history of:
If this patient developed seizures or coma, the condition would be called:
All of the following are components of SEA-3 assessment tool except?
When an EMT begins to assist someone and then leaves the person without providing alternative form of care is referred to as?
While assessing a patient, you note that he is unable to smile or stick his tongue out when asked. This change in neuromotor response is known as what type of apraxia?

You are dispatched to a home for an 85 year old female who is not feeling well. Upon arrival the family member tells you that the patient has been confused for the past one hour. She is disoriented to time place or person, otherwise she is a normally an active person who is alert to time, place, person and event. As part of your differential diagnosis u suspect all of the following except
The mental disorder that is present since birth that is diagnosed during childhood and is based upon the measurement of IQ is referred to as?
The inability to experience pleasure is referred to as?

You are dispatched to evaluate a 67 year old male who is standing in the middle of the local fast food restaurant. He appears disheveled and unkempt. The manager tells you that he is a local homeless man who thinks that something is wrong with him because he calls himself 'God'. When you enter the restaurant u see him standing on the table and telling the customers he is god and has the power to strike them dead. The belief of his power is an example of?

You are dispatched to a call for a 36 year old male construction site worker who is complaining of anxiety like symptoms at work. Upon your arrival the worker tells you that he feels palpitations, chest discomfort, vomiting, nausea, sweating and shaking. 12 lead ECG reveals no changes in relation to myocardial infarction. Based on the presentation of symptoms what is the condition of the patient
Time-limited periods in which one exhibits signs and symptoms of a disorder are called?
Which of the following statements about delirium tremens is not true?
options: They usually develop 2 to 3 days after withdrawal of alcohol
They occur in individuals who have experienced recent binge drinking
DT's are marked by decreased level of consciousness with hallucinations
Seizures and delirium tremens are ominous sign

questions.text: The best approach for gaining information from behavioral emergency patient is to?
options: Physically restrain the patient before questioning

questions.text: Hallucinations, delusions, and disorganized thought, speech and behaviors are commonly associated with which type of psychiatric condition?
Due to which of the following conditions are cardiac abnormalities or dysrhythmias common in patients with eating disorders
options: Bleeding Of Gl tract or esophagus

questions.text: You are dispatched to a local jail for a 20 year old man who has been arrested for destruction of property. Upon arrival the officers inform you that he a long criminal record for fighting and cruelty to animals since adolescence. His crimes have become so aggressive and he appears to have no regard to his or anyone else's safety. Based on this case presentation which personality disorder do u suspect?
Many patients who have lapses in memory will make up events or fill in the gaps with stories about events that never occurred. This process is known as?
